summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a WebSocket connection negotiating permessage-deflate can send a small compressed frame that decompresses to a much larger payload on the server, demonstrating the absence of decompression ratio limits in PerMessageDeflate.java.
expected_security_impact: The exploit should connect to a Tomcat WebSocket endpoint, negotiate permessage-deflate, send a crafted compressed frame, and observe that the decompressed payload size is at least 100 times larger than the compressed wire size, confirming the decompression amplification vulnerability.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Tomcat server from source, started it with the examples webapp, connected to the echo WebSocket endpoint with permessage-deflate, sent a crafted DEFLATE payload, and measured a decompression amplification ratio exceeding 100:1, confirming the server decompresses without ratio limits.
verification_method: The exploit script measures the compressed payload size (bytes sent on the wire) and the decompressed payload size (bytes the server must process). A ratio exceeding 50:1 confirms disproportionate resource consumption. The script also measures round-trip time for compressed vs. uncompressed messages of equivalent decompressed size to demonstrate CPU overhead.
evidence_produced_by_run: The compressed WebSocket frame payload was significantly smaller than the decompressed equivalent (ratio > 100:1).
The server accepted the permessage-deflate extension negotiation without any server-side configuration.
The echo endpoint processed and returned the decompressed data, confirming the full decompression path was exercised.
Round-trip time for compressed messages was measurably higher than for equivalent uncompressed messages, indicating additional CPU work for decompression.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction requires network access to download Tomcat build dependencies during the Docker build phase.
CPU impact measurement is qualitative (timing-based) rather than a hard crash; the vulnerability causes resource exhaustion rather than a binary pass/fail condition.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh copies the target repository source tree on the host and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo for building.
